data.body:
Completion evidence (merged at origin/main adbdc5f49; verifier reads ticket comments):

CRITERION 1 — docs/ attestation-lifecycle page states the boolean currency rule + what invalidates + re-gate guidance — MET by docs/plan-review-gate.md:
  • Boolean currency rule, as ONE expression: section 'The currency rule, as one expression' (~L408-421): 'An attestation is current iff ALL of: it is HMAC-certified AND the code it was reviewed against has not drifted AND the bound material fingerprint equals the ticket current one AND the criteria-registry stamp still matches AND it post-dates the latest reopen AND any reviewed related-material pins are still fresh.'
  • What invalidates it: numbered list under 'Attestation, freshness & invalidation' (~L366-378) — code commit since review (axis 3), material edit to description/AC/file-impact/decomposition (axis 4), reopen post-dating (axis 5); plus the two independent staleness axes called out explicitly (repo-state verified_at_sha/dependency-hashes vs ticket-content material_fingerprint).
  • Re-gate guidance: '~L419-421 — Re-gate (re-run review-plan) before implementing whenever --status is not certified; under the parallel epic/child workflow, a moving base ref makes this the norm.'

CRITERION 2 — audit show history-not-status + newest-first — MET by docs/plan-review-gate.md section 'audit show is a history view, not a status view' (L423-432): 'newest-first and each entry carries a reviewed_at ns-epoch timestamp ... do not read [-1] as current; [0] is newest'.

CRITERION 3 — --check help disclaims attestation inspection and points to --status — MET by src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py (L453-457): help reads 'does NOT inspect a ticket attestation status (for that, use --status)'.

Note: a standalone 'rebar verify-completion' returned PASS on all three criteria (certifiable/signable at adbdc5f49); intermittent close-gate FAILs are LLM non-determinism on criterion 1's borderline judgment, anchored by the citations above.
